Sending Gmail with nodemailer After Google Killed Password Auth (OAuth 2.0)
Nodejs
Node.js
OAuth
Nodemailer

This time I want to add an extra note about nodemailer. Google no longer lets you send Gmail messages with just a username and password, so I'm writing down how to set up Express + OAuth 2.0 + nodemailer + Gmail instead. It's a quick walkthrough — building the OAuth client, grabbing the refresh and access tokens, and wiring them into nodemailer.
/en/nodejs/2019/07/08/nodejs-fullstack-04-middleware-route-guard/
Stop Users Skipping Login With One Express Middleware

We've already covered member registration, login, and how to tell whether someone is logged in. But what if you don't want users jumping straight to the `user` page by typing the URL into the address bar? That's where middleware comes in. In this post I'll show how to gate a whole route behind login with a tiny Express middleware.
/en/nodejs/2019/07/07/nodejs-fullstack-warrior-member-system-02/
Full-Stack Warrior Node.js: Building a Login Flow with Firebase Auth (Member System Part 2)

In the last post we finished the registration logic, so now it's time to build the login feature. This post walks through Firebase's `signInWithEmailAndPassword`, fleshing out the data you collect during signup, extending user fields with the Firebase Realtime Database, and adding server-side validation with `express-validator`.
/en/nodejs/2019/07/07/nodejs-fullstack-warrior-auth-01-firebase-signup/
Building a Member System in Node.js with Firebase Auth (Part 1)

Time to build a member system. Instead of rolling our own auth from scratch, we'll lean on Firebase's authentication. In this first part we set up an Express app, wire it to Firebase, and build a working signup flow that creates real accounts you can see in the Firebase console.
/en/nodejs/2019/07/04/nodejs-fullstack-warrior-03-send-email-deploy-heroku/
Shipping a Mail-Sending App to Heroku (Full-Stack Warrior Node.js, Part 3)

We built a little app that sends emails — now it's time to get it online. This post walks through deploying that mail-sending app to Heroku: installing the Heroku CLI, logging in, pushing your project with Git, setting environment variables, and finally opening the live app. A few small gotchas along the way too.
/en/nodejs/2019/07/04/nodejs-email-heroku-02-wiring-up-packages/
Full-Stack Warrior Node.js: Wiring Up Email Sending and Deploying to Heroku (2)

The basic UI and routes are done, so now it's time to actually wire all those packages we installed into app.js. In this part I'll walk through hooking up express-session, connect-flash, csurf, body-parser, nodemailer, and express-validator one by one — plus a Google-blocking gotcha that'll trip you up when you try to send your first email.
/en/nodejs/2019/07/02/fullstack-warrior-nodejs-01-firebase-express-todolist/
Fullstack Warrior Node.js: Wiring Firebase + Express into a Tiny Todo App (1)

Time to put the pieces together. In this chapter we start combining the Firebase and Express stuff we covered earlier to build a really tiny app — a simple todolist. This first part walks through scaffolding an Express app, connecting it to Firebase Admin SDK, wiring up environment variables, and confirming the connection actually works.
